Objective: every label, actor, message and
note in a rendered diagram is visible in the
which must consult a populated font database
center-fixed fisheye over the rendered diagram — the
centre bulges/magnifies and content compresses smoothly
toward the edges. Layered ON TOP of the existing
scroll-to-zoom global zoom; drag still pans to bring a
region into the magnified centre. Realized by
The toolbar no longer has Reset / Zoom + / Zoom -
buttons. Zoom is scroll-only (global, at cursor);
the fisheye is the focus mechanism on top of it.
an inner-edge chevron handle; clicking it collapses
the panel to a thin sliver, clicking the sliver
MddViewer registers a bundled Inter (UI) and
set, then on every frame asks dark-light for
the OS appearance and applies catppuccin_egui
rounding. No new SVG rendering, no new model
state — chrome only. Realized by SEQ-APPLY-THEME
Objective (USE-VIEW-DEPLOY): the left rail gains a
THIRD source. Today MddViewer::new() loads exactly
rasterization target), so it never appears in the
interactive viewer. Objective: a dedicated DEPLOY
It stays OUT of ModelRegistry: /mdd-deploy is a
utility outside the parity gate, so /mdd-validate
toggle row gains a "Source" button between "Diagram"
and "Diff". Selecting it puts the viewer in
View::Source, which paints the selected model file's
directives highlighted — instead of the rendered SVG.
No new rendering: the text is read straight from disk
(analogous to the OCL Source sub-mode, which never
depends on a rendered artifact — OCL-PUML-SOURCE-NO-RENDER).
Toggling back to "Diagram" repaints the SVG canvas.
Realized by SEQ-VIEW-PUML-SOURCE; state held in
